verdict.summary: Your application has critical issues that should be addressed. Review the issues section for details.

groups.description: Problems with DDD tactical patterns that affect domain integrity

violations.title: Domain purity violation
violations.message: Domain type 'Customer' has 2 forbidden infrastructure import(s)
violations.impact: Infrastructure imports in the domain layer (e.g., JPA annotations, Spring framework classes) couple the business logic to specific technologies. This makes the domain model harder to test in isolation, impossible to reuse with different frameworks, and blurs the separation between what the system does and how it does it.

suggestion.action: Remove infrastructure imports and use domain-pure alternatives
steps: Identify all forbidden infrastructure imports in the domain type
Replace framework annotations with domain concepts (e.g., remove @Entity, @Column)
If infrastructure behavior is needed, define a port interface in the domain layer
Move framework-specific code to infrastructure adapters

violations.title: Entity without identity
violations.message: Entity 'Customer' has no identity field
violations.impact: Entities are defined by their identity, not their attributes. Without an identity field, the entity cannot be uniquely identified, tracked, or persisted. Equality comparisons become attribute-based instead of identity-based, which breaks DDD semantics and can cause subtle bugs in collections and persistence operations.

suggestion.action: Add a dedicated identity field to the entity
steps: Create a typed identifier (e.g., OrderId wrapping UUID) or use a simple ID type
Add the identity field to the entity class
Implement equals/hashCode based on the identity field (or use a record)

violations.title: Port direction violation
violations.message: DRIVEN port 'CustomerRepository' is not used by any application service
violations.impact: Ports should only be used in the intended direction. Driving ports should be called from external adapters, and driven ports should be called from the domain.

suggestion.action: Correct the port usage direction
steps: Review which component is calling the port
Move the call to the appropriate layer
Consider if the port type is correct

violations.title: Driven port without adapter
violations.message: Driven port 'CustomerRepository' [REPOSITORY] has no adapter implementation
violations.impact: Ports without adapters cannot be used. The application cannot interact with external systems or be invoked by driving adapters.

suggestion.action: Create a driven adapter implementing this port as a JPA persistence layer
steps: Create JPA entity mapping the aggregate
Create mapper to convert between domain and JPA entity
Create JPA repository adapter implementing the port
Add identity converters if using typed identifiers
Register the implementation with your DI container

violations.title: Layer isolation violation
violations.message: DOMAIN type 'InventoryService' depends on PORT type 'ProductRepository' (not allowed)
violations.impact: Layer isolation violations break the hexagonal architecture contract. When layers bypass ports to access each other directly, the architecture loses its ability to swap implementations and maintain testability.

suggestion.action: Route the dependency through the appropriate port
steps: Identify the direct cross-layer dependency
Create or use an existing port interface for this interaction
Refactor the caller to depend on the port instead of the concrete class
Ensure the adapter implements the port on the infrastructure side

remediation.summary: 5 actions required. Manual effort: 47,5 days. With HexaGlue: 29,5 days (saves 18,0 days).

actions.title: Add a dedicated identity field to the entity
actions.description: Entities are defined by their identity, not their attributes. Without an identity field, the entity cannot be uniquely identified, tracked, or persisted. Equality comparisons become attribute-based instead of identity-based, which breaks DDD semantics and can cause subtle bugs in collections and persistence operations.

actions.title: Remove infrastructure imports and use domain-pure alternatives
actions.description: Infrastructure imports in the domain layer (e.g., JPA annotations, Spring framework classes) couple the business logic to specific technologies. This makes the domain model harder to test in isolation, impossible to reuse with different frameworks, and blurs the separation between what the system does and how it does it.

actions.title: Correct the port usage direction
actions.description: Ports should only be used in the intended direction. Driving ports should be called from external adapters, and driven ports should be called from the domain.

actions.title: Create a driven adapter implementing this port as a JPA persistence layer
actions.description: Ports without adapters cannot be used. The application cannot interact with external systems or be invoked by driving adapters.

actions.title: Route the dependency through the appropriate port
actions.description: Layer isolation violations break the hexagonal architecture contract. When layers bypass ports to access each other directly, the architecture loses its ability to swap implementations and maintain testability.
